title: Classic Soft Pretzels
description: Golden, chewy soft pretzels with a perfectly salted crust — bakery quality right from your own oven. Crave Coarse Pretzel Salt stays crisp and white through the bake.
ingredients: Crave Coarse Pretzel Salt, for topping
1 large egg yolk + 1 tbsp water (egg wash)
instructions: In a large bowl, combine warm water, yeast, and sugar. Let sit 5 minutes until foamy.
Add melted butter and kosher salt to the yeast mixture. Gradually mix in flour until a dough forms.
Knead dough on a lightly floured surface for 8 minutes until smooth and elastic. Place in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise 1 hour.
Preheat oven to 450°F. Line two baking sheets with parchment and grease well.
Bring 10 cups of water and baking soda to a rolling boil in a large pot.
Divide dough into 8 equal pieces. Roll each into a 24-inch rope, then shape into a pretzel.
Boil each pretzel in the baking soda bath for 30 seconds per side. Remove with a slotted spatula.
Place on prepared baking sheets. Brush with egg wash and sprinkle generously with Crave Coarse Pretzel Salt.
Bake 12–14 minutes until deep golden brown. Serve warm with mustard or cheese dip.
tip: Don't skip the baking soda bath — it's what gives soft pretzels their iconic chewy crust and deep brown color. Crave Pretzel Salt won't dissolve or turn grey in the oven, so your pretzels look as good as they taste.

title: Homemade Apple Pie Filling (Canning Safe)
description: A thick, perfectly spiced apple pie filling that's safe for water-bath canning. Clear Jel is the only starch approved for home canning — it creates a stable, clear gel that won't break down during processing.
ingredients: 12 cups peeled, cored, sliced apples (about 9 medium)
¾ cup Crave Clear Jel Powder (cook-type)
instructions: Sterilize 7 quart jars and keep them hot. Prepare lids and bands according to manufacturer's instructions.
In a large pot, combine sugar, Crave Clear Jel Powder, cinnamon, nutmeg, and salt. Whisk together.
Add apple juice and cold water to the dry mixture. Stir until the Clear Jel is fully dissolved.
Cook over medium-high heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and begins to bubble.
Add lemon juice and stir well. Fold in the sliced apples.
Return mixture to a boil, stirring gently, for 1 minute.
Ladle hot filling into hot quart jars, leaving 1-inch headspace. Remove air bubbles.
Wipe jar rims clean. Apply lids and bands finger-tight.
Process in a boiling water bath canner for 25 minutes. Remove and let cool 12–24 hours before storing.
tip: Use only Crave Cook-Type Clear Jel for canning — never cornstarch or instant Clear Jel. Cook-type Clear Jel is the only starch that remains safe and stable through heat processing, which is why it's recommended by the USDA for home canning.

title: Home-Cured Bacon
description: Real, old-fashioned cured and smoked bacon made at home. Crave Pink Curing Salt #1 (Prague Powder) gives you the authentic pink color, classic bacon flavor, and — most importantly — food-safe preservation.
ingredients: 1 tsp Crave Pink Curing Salt #1 (Prague Powder #1)
¼ cup brown sugar or maple sugar
Wood chips for smoking (hickory, apple, or cherry)
instructions: Weigh your pork belly precisely. Calculate Pink Curing Salt at 1 tsp per 5 lb of meat (or 0.25% by weight) — measure carefully.
In a small bowl, combine Crave Pink Curing Salt #1, kosher salt, brown sugar, and all spices. Mix well.
Rub the cure mixture all over the pork belly, coating all surfaces evenly.
Place belly in a large zip-lock bag or vacuum seal bag. Refrigerate for 5–7 days.
Flip the bag once a day to redistribute the cure. The belly will release liquid — this is normal.
After curing, rinse the belly under cold water and pat dry. Let it rest uncovered in the refrigerator overnight to form a pellicle (sticky surface).
Smoke the belly at 200–225°F until it reaches an internal temperature of 150°F (about 2–3 hours depending on thickness).
Let cool, then slice to your preferred thickness. Refrigerate up to 7 days or freeze up to 3 months.
tip: Always use a kitchen scale to measure Pink Curing Salt — precision matters for food safety. More is not better. Crave Pink Curing Salt #1 is formulated at 6.25% sodium nitrite, the industry standard for short-term curing projects under 30 days.

title: Homemade Beef Jerky
description: Tender, flavorful beef jerky with a savory-sweet marinade. Using Crave Pink Curing Salt ensures your jerky is safe to eat and gives it that classic deep color and preserved texture.
ingredients: 2 lb beef eye of round or flank steak
¼ tsp Crave Pink Curing Salt #1
instructions: Trim all visible fat from the beef (fat causes spoilage). Slice against the grain into ¼-inch strips.
In a bowl, whisk together all marinade ingredients including Crave Pink Curing Salt #1.
Add beef strips to the marinade, toss to coat completely. Cover and refrigerate 8–24 hours.
Preheat your oven to 170°F (or as low as it will go) with the rack in the top position.
Remove beef from marinade, pat lightly dry, and arrange on wire racks set over foil-lined baking sheets.
Prop the oven door open slightly with a wooden spoon to allow moisture to escape.
Dry in oven for 3–4 hours, flipping once halfway, until jerky is dry but still slightly flexible.
Let cool completely before storing in airtight containers. Keeps at room temperature up to 2 weeks.
tip: For the best texture, slice the beef partially frozen — it makes uniform ¼-inch slices much easier. The Pink Curing Salt not only adds food safety but also gives jerky its characteristic deep mahogany color that plain dried meat lacks.

title: Pretzel Bagels
description: The best of both worlds — the chewy, dense texture of a bagel meets the dark, malty crust of a soft pretzel. Finished with Crave Coarse Pretzel Salt for the perfect crunch.
ingredients: Crave Coarse Pretzel Salt, for topping
instructions: Dissolve yeast and sugar in warm water. Let stand 5–8 minutes until foamy.
Mix in flour, fine salt, and oil. Knead until a smooth, stiff dough forms — about 10 minutes.
Divide into 8 equal pieces, roll into smooth balls, then poke a hole in the center and stretch into bagel rings about 4 inches across.
Place on a floured surface, cover, and rest 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 425°F. Bring baking soda and water to a boil in a large, wide pot.
Boil each bagel for 30 seconds per side. They should float to the top.
Transfer to parchment-lined baking sheets. Brush generously with beaten egg.
Sprinkle Crave Coarse Pretzel Salt over each bagel immediately after brushing.
Bake 20–22 minutes until deep brown and crispy. Cool at least 15 minutes before slicing.
tip: Bread flour gives pretzel bagels their signature dense, chewy bite — all-purpose flour works in a pinch but the texture won't be quite as satisfying. Crave Pretzel Salt holds up beautifully through the high baking temperature without melting or discoloring.

title: TVP Vegan Tacos
description: Savory, protein-packed tacos made with Crave TVP. Seasoned just like ground beef, these plant-based tacos are so satisfying that even meat-eaters will go back for seconds.
ingredients: 1½ cups Crave Textured Vegetable Protein (TVP), dry
Toppings: diced tomato, shredded cabbage, avocado, lime, salsa, cilantro
instructions: Pour hot vegetable broth over dry Crave TVP in a bowl. Stir, cover, and let rehydrate for 10 minutes.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add diced onion and cook until softened, about 4 minutes.
Add garlic and cook 1 minute more until fragrant.
Drain any excess liquid from the rehydrated TVP, then add to the skillet.
Stir in all spices — chili powder, cumin, paprika, and oregano. Season with salt and pepper.
Cook, stirring frequently, for 5–7 minutes until TVP is lightly browned and well-seasoned.
Warm tortillas in a dry skillet or directly over a gas flame.
Build tacos: spoon TVP mixture into tortillas, add your favorite toppings, and finish with a squeeze of lime.
tip: For the best texture, let the TVP get a little brown and crispy in the skillet before serving — this mimics the texture of browned ground beef. Rehydrating in broth instead of water adds depth of flavor that makes all the difference.

title: Keto Cream Sauce
description: A silky, restaurant-quality cream sauce thickened with Crave Xanthan Gum instead of flour — zero carbs, full flavor. Works beautifully over chicken, pasta, vegetables, or as a base for any cream sauce.
ingredients: ½ cup chicken or vegetable broth
¼ tsp Crave Pure Xanthan Gum
Salt and white pepper to taste
Fresh parsley or chives, to garnish
instructions: Melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Add garlic and sauté for 1 minute until fragrant — don't let it brown.
Pour in heavy cream and broth. Bring to a gentle simmer.
In a small bowl, whisk ¼ tsp Crave Xanthan Gum with 1 tbsp cold water to form a slurry.
Whisk the xanthan slurry into the simmering cream mixture. The sauce will thicken within 1–2 minutes.
Reduce heat to low. Stir in Parmesan until fully melted and smooth.
Season with salt and white pepper. If the sauce is too thick, thin with a splash of broth.
Serve immediately over chicken, zucchini noodles, vegetables, or steak.
tip: A little Crave Xanthan Gum goes a very long way — start with less than you think you need. Unlike flour or cornstarch, xanthan gum works in cold or hot liquid, creates a stable sauce that won't break or separate, and adds zero carbs or flavor of its own.

title: Peach Preserves
description: Bright, sun-ripened peach preserves with a thick, glossy set that holds up beautifully in the jar. Clear Jel gives these preserves a clean, clear look that traditional pectin can't match.
ingredients: 4 cups peeled, pitted, chopped fresh peaches (about 8 medium)
¼ cup Crave Clear Jel Powder (cook-type)
¼ tsp almond extract (optional but wonderful)
instructions: Sterilize 6 half-pint jars and keep hot. Prepare lids and bands.
In a large heavy-bottomed pot, combine peaches and 1½ cups of the sugar. Let macerate 30 minutes to draw out juices.
In a small bowl, whisk remaining ½ cup sugar with Crave Clear Jel Powder until combined.
Bring the peach mixture to a boil over medium-high heat, stirring frequently.
Gradually whisk in the Clear Jel and sugar mixture, stirring constantly to prevent lumps.
Add lemon juice, almond extract if using, cinnamon, and salt. Stir well.
Return to a full rolling boil and cook 2 minutes, stirring constantly, until thick and glossy.
Ladle into hot jars, leaving ¼-inch headspace. Process in boiling water bath for 10 minutes.
tip: Cook-type Clear Jel creates preserves with a beautiful, clear, glossy appearance that looks stunning in the jar — far more professional than the cloudy set you get with regular pectin. It also sets more firmly, so your preserves spread perfectly without being runny.
